text:
By Henry Moore

Adam Peaty won silver in the 100m breaststroke final - after Italian Nicol脙虏 Martinenghi denied Team GB their first gold in Paris by two-hundredths of a second.

Adam Peaty paid tribute to partner Holly Ramsay after testing positive for Covid just hours after winning silver in the 100m breaststroke at Paris 2024.

Peaty swam 59.05 - but was pipped by Nicol脙虏 Martinenghi who swam 59.03 to win gold in the pool.

The Olympic record-holder had suggested he felt unwell after securing second place and his symptoms only worsened after the final.

芒聙聹Adam Peaty began feeling unwell on Sunday, ahead of his Men芒聙聶s 100m Breaststroke final,芒聙聺 Team GB said in a statement.

芒聙聹In the hours after the final, his symptoms became worse and he was tested for COVID early on Monday morning. He tested positive at that point.

Read more:Olympics LIVE: Team GB wins two golds on Day 3 of Paris Olympics

Read more:Becky Downie emotional as she makes return to Olympics after 8 years after overcoming family tragedy

芒聙聹He is hopeful to be back in competition for the relay events later in the swimming programme.

芒聙聹As in any case of illness, the situation is being managed appropriately, with all usual precautions being taken to keep the wider delegation healthy.芒聙聺

Taking to Instagram in the wake of the news, Peaty said: "A night full of raw emotion and sport in its true form.

"These last 14 months have been incredibly testing and I do not regret one training session or decision I made.

"I芒聙聶ve continued to fight and find new ways to enjoy something that has broken me to the core and to end up with an Olympic silver through all of that is an absolute blessing.

"I芒聙聶m more proud of the man and athlete I am from last night than I have been across my entire career."

This diagnosis casts doubt over Peaty芒聙聶s hopes of winning more medals at the Paris 2024 games.

If able to recover in time, the swimmer would play a key role in Great Britain芒聙聶s men芒聙聶s and mixed 4x100m medley relay quartets.

The men芒聙聶s final is due to take place on August 4, while the mixed race is scheduled for the day before.

Speaking after his silver medal win, Peaty admitted to feeling unwell: 芒聙聹I芒聙聶ve had a bit of a curveball with my throat but, please, it芒聙聶s no excuse at all.

芒聙聹But it芒聙聶s a curveball I芒聙聶ve had to respond to.

芒聙聹You can train eight years for something and not feel 100 per cent on the day and (not being) 100 per cent costs you 0.02 seconds, that芒聙聶s just the way it is.

芒聙聹I芒聙聶m so happy to pass the baton to Nicolo because I芒聙聶ve been racing him for so long. All I could see in my eyes were Qin and Arno.

芒聙聹They are two of the best executors in that final, but that芒聙聶s just sport. I got a little bit blindsided, I touched the wall and I truly believed I got it but it wasn芒聙聶t meant to be.芒聙聺
